title: next_diversifier(): atomic increment and persist diversifier index
description: Implement next_diversifier(account: u32) -> Result<DiversifierIndex> in WalletStore. Must: read current index from DB, check for overflow (DiversifierIndex is 11 bytes, max value ~2^88 — in practice we use u128 for storage and error if > 2^88-1), increment, persist in a single UPDATE, return the NEW index (post-increment, not the old one). Concurrency: SQLite single-writer model means two callers in the same process cannot race, but use BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent TOCTOU with an external writer. Defensive programming: if the account row doesn't exist, return Err (not silently create one); if the index is at u128::MAX (practically unreachable), return Err with a clear message. Use a subagent to read WalletStore and understand the existing transaction pattern before writing the SQL.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: 10 consecutive calls return [0,1,2,...,9] (or [1,2,...,10] depending on pre/post convention — choose pre-increment). Test: persisted value survives WalletStore::open() reconnect. Test: overflow returns Err, not panic.
close_reason: next_diversifier() implemented in WalletStore with transaction-wrapped read-then-update. Pre-increment semantics: returns current index, stores current+1. Max = 2^88-1 (ZIP-32 11-byte diversifier limit). 4 tests: sequential [0..9], missing account Err, survives reopen, overflow Err.
